artificer-primary plus sorcerer-multiclass actor does not gain secondary-class saving throw proficiencies on actor root

verify Dauligor feat selection works from a real feat catalog during ASI prompt flow
verify multiclass HP behavior after the max-override fix, especially second-class imports and damaged actors; max HP overwrite is fixed but current HP still needs follow-up

verify ItemChoice and Subclass advancement rows behave correctly in live UI after the latest sync fix

nextGoals: build a creator-facing reference model around native Foundry dnd5e terminology
update Dauligor class and character sheets to display and explain Foundry-style references while authoring
bring character-sheet advancement authoring back in line with the current Foundry-ready advancement model
remove or replace deprecated advancement-authoring paths in the app-side creator flow
appAlignment.highestPriorityAfterReferences: phase out deprecated advancement-authoring paths in the app-side creator flow
notes: CharacterBuilder.tsx is the most likely entry point for creator-facing character-sheet behavior
characterExport.ts is the likely convergence point for Foundry-oriented reference serialization or formula normalization
AdvancementManager.tsx is the current app-side advancement editor and should be compared against the Foundry-ready advancement shape first
SpellcastingAdvancementManager.tsx already exposes native-style formula examples and may inform reference-helper UX
SubclassEditor.tsx already has explicit deprecated-feature handling, which is a strong signal that advancement cleanup should include deprecated app-side class and subclass authoring paths
referenceSyntax.ts now provides first-wave semantic-to-Foundry preview logic for app-side helper surfaces
ReferenceSyntaxHelp.tsx now uses a compact side popout instead of a large inline block and is wired into class and subclass spellcasting formula fields plus limited-use feature formula fields

ReferenceSheetDialog.tsx now provides a draggable full reference utility window and is linked from class/subclass save areas, feature modal headers, advancement dialog headers, and the character sheet launcher card
CharacterReferencePanel.tsx is now a compact CharacterBuilder launcher card instead of a permanently expanded table
spellcasting formula authoring now supports contextual shortcuts: @level, @totalLevel, @prof, @mod, @value, plus min()/floor()/ceil() guidance
class export normalization now resolves those spellcasting shortcuts into Foundry-native formula paths using the current class identifier and selected ability
AdvancementManager.tsx now canonicalizes editor state on every modal update instead of relying on save-time repair for deprecated advancement shapes
touched advancement rows now save canonical allowReplacements, sizes, trait choice source, and item-choice count source data
classExport.ts now derives a compatibility size field from canonical sizes data during export so Foundry payloads remain stable
advancementState.ts now provides shared canonical advancement normalization for app-side editor and creator flows
ClassEditor.tsx and SubclassEditor.tsx now normalize root and feature advancement arrays when loading, editing, and saving
CharacterBuilder.tsx now normalizes cached class, subclass, and feature advancements before progression logic consumes them
CharacterBuilder.tsx now derives trait choices from canonical options/choiceCount data instead of only the older configuration.choices shape
CharacterBuilder.tsx now distinguishes primary-class level 1 from multiclass level 1 when interpreting base class advancements

CharacterBuilder.tsx now re-applies class profile trait grants and selected trait choices onto live sheet state through classGrantedTraits so class saves, skills, armor, weapons, tools, and languages appear on the character
CharacterBuilder.tsx now skips base starting-equipment ItemChoice rows for multiclass class onboarding and preserves classId alongside className in new progression entries

CharacterBuilder.tsx no longer fuzzy-attaches advancement cards to feature text by matching names or identifiers; level advancements now render as their own standalone sections beneath the feature list

CharacterBuilder.tsx Character Sheet tab now derives a class progression summary from cached class/subclass features and scaling columns, showing per-class granted features, current scale values, and selected advancement options
advancementState.ts canonical trait choice helpers now preserve categoryIds so builder dialogs can resolve both explicit pools and category-backed trait collections
the next pass should extend reference helpers into more formula-bearing editor surfaces and continue deprecated advancement-authoring cleanup beyond AdvancementManager.tsx
the current inline usage formula helper is useful but too space-heavy; future reference help should prefer a button, hover target, side popout, or dedicated reference window
spellcasting authoring needs more room and should not feel cramped by the reference helper sharing the same narrow column
the dedicated reference sheet should eventually include sections for core information, attributes, skills, class features, class columns, and later effects
class references must key by stable identifier rather than display name so duplicate class names on a sheet do not break resolution
class-column references must resolve from linked ScaleValue / scaling identifiers rather than only from the column label
future effects/reference work should review Foundry wiki guidance and Midi-QOL effect conventions

uniqueOptionGroupEditor.itemListPattern: compact divide-y rows with hover-reveal edit/delete buttons, matching ClassEditor feature list
uniqueOptionGroupEditor.itemEditPattern: Dialog modal max-w-4xl, consistent with ClassEditor feature modal
uniqueOptionGroupEditor.groupDetailsClassRestrictions: searchable multi-select with chip display, search input, scrollable filtered list

advancementManager.classIdProp: optional classId from ClassEditor (id) or SubclassEditor (classId || parentClass?.id)
advancementManager.optionGroupFiltering: shows groups where classIds is empty or includes classId
advancementManager.crossClassGroupSearch: inline max-h-40 panel, no absolute positioning, to avoid overflow clipping in modal containers
advancementManager.allFeaturesFetch: lazy fetch on modal open, cached in allFeatures state
canonicalAuthoringShapeNotes: trait rows now author allowReplacements instead of allowReplacement
size rows now author sizes as the editor source of truth
item pools, optional pools, and excluded option ids are deduped in editor state
trait choiceSource and item countSource are normalized inside the modal instead of only on save
advancementManager.sharedNormalizer: src/lib/advancementState.ts is now the common source for canonical advancement normalization across editors and creator flows

markdownEditor.bugDescription: TipTap WYSIWYG mode never received async-loaded values because the sync useEffect was gated on !isWYSIWYG
markdownEditor.fix: removed !isWYSIWYG guard; emitUpdate: false on setContent prevents feedback loops

characterBuilderSheetAlignment.sharedProgressionSummarySource: CharacterBuilder.tsx now builds a per-class progression summary used by both the sheet and the class-step timeline
progressionSummaryIncludes: raw feature fallback when no ItemGrant-backed feature records exist
granted non-feature items from class, subclass, and feature-owned ItemGrant rows
classTimelineStandaloneGrantSections: granted items render separately from feature cards
recentBuilderCleanup: removed the duplicate nested Character Info header inside the sheet info column so the top sheet subtab bar is the only Character Info / Features / Spells navigation control

class-profile trait synthesis now mirrors fixed values into both fixed and grants, and trait collection now reads grants as well as fixed so base class saving throws resolve correctly in builder state

progression summaries now rehydrate feature and granted-item names from the shared feature and option caches before rendering or persisting owned state so raw ids stop leaking into builder class timelines and the sheet features tab

AbilityScoreImprovement advancements now surface as explicit level-advancement cards in the class timeline and open the existing ability-score management modal; deeper ASI choice persistence is still a follow-up
the upper character sheet now stays visible while only the existing lower md:col-span-2 pane swaps between Character Info, Features, and Spells
the builder now preloads grant-linked uniqueOptionItems so granted-item displays can resolve names after reload instead of staying on raw document ids
the builder now loads armor, weapon, tool, language, and matching category labels on sheet load and uses them for human-readable proficiency and trait-choice displays

classMigrationDirection.backendGoal: saved/exported class data should converge on canonical advancement-driven progression truth even if editor fields remain convenience inputs
knownEditorIssues: multiclass proficiency category headers currently do not work because toggleGroup only updates proficiencies state
proficiency display sync currently expands category selections into individual item names instead of preferring category labels
preferredDisplaySyncRules: whole-category fixed selections should display the category label when possible
choice rows should prefer phrasing like '{Choice Number} {Category} of your choice'
if appended after existing fixed text, use '; and {Choice Number} {Category} of your choice'
classMigrationDirection.spellcastingMigrationNote: before turning spellcasting into a more progression-linked backend model, compare local Foundry dnd5e and Plutonium expectations

implementedEditorFixes: multiclass proficiency category headers now update multiclassProficiencies instead of the main proficiencies state
grouped proficiency display sync now prefers whole-category labels over expanding full categories into individual item names
choice-oriented grouped display sync now generates phrasing such as '{Choice Number} {Category} of your choice' and appends it after fixed text when needed
handleSave fallback display generation now reuses the same grouped sync helper as the Sync buttons
category-backed proficiency overlap is now allowed for armor, weapons, tools, and languages so a fixed item can coexist with a category choice pool
latestClassMigrationPass.nextBackendStep: review and extend the shared canonical class progression builder into adjacent subclass and builder-owned-state flows
exportReview: generated class JSON shows canonical base rows and implicit ItemGrant rows from the shared progression builder
classExport.ts now preserves armorDisplayName, weaponsDisplayName, and toolsDisplayName inside exported proficiency blocks
classPreviewFollowup: ClassList.tsx tools preview now prefers the saved toolsDisplayName string before rebuilding from raw tool category/item state
existing exported source files need to be regenerated to include the new preserved display-name fields

implemented: buildCanonicalSubclassProgression now owns canonical subclass custom-advancement filtering and optional implicit subclass feature grants
SubclassEditor save now writes canonical subclass custom advancements through the shared helper
classExport subclass export now uses the shared subclass progression helper instead of a direct implicit-grant branch
resolvedPolicy: subclasses should not gain generated base progression rows beyond their reference to the parent class; the parent class already defines the subclass feature schedule
subclass features should always be granted at their authored levels once a subclass is selected, like main-class features
subclass custom advancements must be allowed at non-subclass levels such as 5, 11, and 17; only the main subclass feature schedule stays locked to the parent class
subclass spellcasting should participate in the same overall spellcasting system as main-class spellcasting
subclass spellcasting should contribute to multiclass slot progression exactly the same way Foundry/dnd5e does, using the admin multiclass master chart plus formula-mapped casting contribution
subclassProgressionAlignment.resolvedExample: Wizard 3 contributes 3 casting levels and Fighter 3 (Eldritch Knight) contributes ceil(3 / 3) = 1, so Wizard 3 / Fighter 3 (Eldritch Knight) has total spellcasting level 4

implemented: CharacterBuilder.tsx now treats progression entries as the source of truth for per-class subclassId ownership
builder save now persists derived classes summaries from grouped progression entries while mirroring top-level classId and subclassId for compatibility
subclass selection in the class step now writes the chosen subclass onto matching progression entries for that class
builder trait-grant and class progression summaries now resolve subclass documents per class group instead of only through the global subclass field

sheet spellcasting now computes multiclass slot progression from all active class and subclass spellcasting contributors using spellcastingTypes formulas plus the standardMulticlassProgression master chart
generatePairingJson now derives class and subclass items from grouped progression data instead of the stale character.classes field and now includes subclass spellcasting blocks
selected advancement options in CharacterBuilder.tsx now use parent-scoped keys instead of plain _id-level keys, with scope built from parent type plus stable class/subclass/feature identifiers
builder selection writes now clean up matching legacy _id-level keys when an advancement choice is edited

the builder no longer reads legacy _id-level advancement-selection keys and instead shows a blocking warning telling the user to go back to class progression and reselect those choices before export or JSON preview
characterBuilderProgressionAlignment.discrepancyToRevisit: existing saved characters can still carry legacy _id-level advancement-selection keys until those choices are manually reselected or a future explicit migration rewrites them eagerly
progressionOwnedStatePass1: CharacterBuilder.tsx now persists progressionState on the character document

progressionState.classPackages is now synchronized from grouped progression entries and stores per-class class identity, subclass identity, class level, introduction mode, and per-class advancementSelections
classPackages currently also reserve grantedFeatureRefs, grantedItemRefs, spellcasting, hitPointHistory, and scaleState fields for the next builder phases
builder reads advancement selections from progressionState.classPackages first and mirrors a non-legacy top-level selectedOptions map from that package-backed state for compatibility
handleSave now rebuilds and persists both progressionState.classPackages and the mirrored scoped selectedOptions map from the package-backed state
progressionOwnedStatePass2: progressionState.ownedFeatures is now synchronized from class/subclass progression summaries and stores canonical granted feature ownership by class/subclass
progressionState.ownedItems is now synchronized from granted non-feature progression entries plus selected option-item advancement choices
each class package now also stores synchronized grantedFeatureRefs and grantedItemRefs
sheet-side progression summaries now prefer persisted ownedFeatures and ownedItems instead of only reconstructing granted ownership from raw class data every render
generatePairingJson now exports class and subclass feat items from canonical owned feature state and also exports selected class-option items as feat items using their semantic/source-id metadata
moduleCompatibilityBoundary: actor bundle shape remains actor plus items[]
class/subclass items still own advancement truth
exported owned feature-like items keep flags.dauligor-pairing.sourceId-style metadata the module can interpret
broader inventory/equipment-style owned items are not yet emitted into the actor bundle until their import contract is ready
characterExporterRefactor: src/lib/characterExport.ts now builds a direct dauligor.actor-bundle.v1 payload instead of the older actor-root-heavy single-class snapshot shape
the exporter now emits actor root, embedded class items, embedded subclass items, owned feature feat items, and selected option feat items from canonical progression-owned state
class and subclass advancement values now come from package-backed advancement selections instead of the old top-level single-class shortcut
embedded export items keep module-compatible flags.dauligor-pairing.sourceId-style metadata so the current actor import flow can still interpret them
exportReviewFixes: characterExport.ts now derives actor proficiency bonus from total character level instead of trusting stale stored proficiencyBonus values
actor bundle flags now use primaryClassId and primarySubclassId plus progressionClassIds and progressionSubclassIds instead of misleading singular multiclass classId and subclassId flags
class and subclass actor-bundle spellcasting blocks now export a clean progression none shape when hasSpellcasting is false instead of carrying active-looking defaults
class actor-bundle primaryAbility values are now flattened and normalized instead of exporting nested arrays such as [["STR"]]
ClassEditor.tsx now saves normalized lowercase primaryAbility and primaryAbilityChoice arrays and deletes the spellcasting field entirely when spellcasting is disabled
SubclassEditor.tsx now deletes the spellcasting field entirely when spellcasting is disabled
CharacterBuilder.tsx now derives and persists proficiency bonus from total character level
builder save/export now omit hp.max when it only matches derivedHpMax so class advancements remain the source of truth unless there is a real max-HP override
builder sheet HP display now falls back to derivedHpMax when there is no stored max override
characterBuilderProgressionAlignment.nextProgressionOwnedFollowup: ownedSpells is still only a reserved shell, and broader inventory/equipment ownership still needs a formal export contract before those owned items should be emitted into the actor bundle

recommendedSource: native Foundry spell item JSON export
Foundry-side Item.toObject() dump of spell documents
spellImportResearch.notRecommendedSource: raw installed dnd5e packs/spells and packs/spells24 folders because they are LevelDB-style pack stores rather than plain JSON
importerMappingStillNeededFor: casting shell fields such as activation, range, duration, and target

implemented: module export-service.js now builds dauligor.foundry-spell-folder-export.v1 payloads for Item folders containing native Foundry spell items
payload includes game metadata, folder metadata, aggregate summary counts, and a spells array
each spell entry includes convenience metadata plus the authoritative native Foundry sourceDocument from spell.toObject()
main.js now exposes an Item Directory sidebar Export Spell Folder button that prompts for a spell folder and optional subfolder inclusion
foundrySpellFolderExporter.nextStep: build app-side batch import and single-spell review from dauligor.foundry-spell-folder-export.v1, with a 5etools-style spell list/detail layout

implemented: src/lib/spellImport.ts now parses dauligor.foundry-spell-folder-export.v1 payloads into import candidates and normalized spell save payloads
spell import parsing now matches Foundry source book abbreviations to Dauligor source records and flags unresolved source mappings
import candidates preserve the authoritative Foundry sourceDocument along with foundryShell and foundryImport metadata for later export/verification work

src/components/compendium/SpellImportWorkbench.tsx now provides multi-file JSON upload, batch stats, search/filter list browsing, 5etools-style spell detail display, single-spell import, and visible-batch import
src/pages/compendium/SpellsEditor.tsx now exposes Foundry Import and Manual Editor tabs
spell import candidates now normalize relative Foundry image paths such as icons/... to https://images.dauligor.com/... for app-side display and saved imageUrl fields

the spell import workbench now fetches spell tag groups and tags, allows per-spell tag assignment before import, persists tagIds on saved spell records, and supports tag-based filtering in the left browser column
methodNormalization: spell preparation mode handling now uses Foundry-native method values
the spell editor default preparation mode is now spell instead of the old incorrect prepared value
nextRefinementAreas: allow manual source override for unresolved spell sources if needed
grow the browser/detail pane to include richer list metadata such as classes, subclasses, feats, and tags when those become available
decide whether to expand the manual spell editor surface for activation/range/duration/target or continue preserving those primarily under foundryShell

searchAndFilterUi: custom spell-tag filters from Tag Manager
filterShell: SpellList now reuses the shared src/components/compendium/FilterBar.tsx window shell
FilterBar.tsx now supports custom filter content and labels so spell filters can use the same modal shell as ClassList
preferred spell-filter styling follows the ClassList filter language: Advanced Filters title, class-style section headers, Include All/Clear controls, and filter-tag/btn-gold option buttons
listLayout: left pane is a compact spell table
columns are name, level, school, and source abbreviation
detailLayout: right pane is a user-facing spell detail view based on the importer display shell
activities and effects are preserved in saved spell data but not emphasized in this reader view
imageLoadingPolicy: no spell icons render in list rows
no spell is auto-selected on initial load
only the selected spell renders an img tag in the detail pane
SpellArtPreview.tsx now preloads the next image and shows a ClassView-style spinner instead of leaving the old spell image visible while the new one loads
uiSizing: spell filter modal now uses a broader class-list-style shell with max-w-[95vw] md:max-w-4xl and tall centered card treatment
spell detail art now renders at 126x126 to match the native icon size
spell detail image wrapper is explicitly 126x126 with overflow-hidden and the img is display:block so the icon does not leave an inline gap below the art
managerPages: SpellsEditor.tsx now provides a Back To Spells link to /compendium/spells
SpellsEditor.tsx now includes an admin-only Purge All Spells action for full reimport/reset workflows
spell purge now calls the server route api/admin/spells/purge.ts with the signed-in Firebase bearer token instead of attempting browser-side Firestore batch deletes
api/_lib/firebase-admin.ts now exposes requireAdminAccess(...) for admin-only server actions beyond the R2 image manager flow
src/lib/spellAdminApi.ts now wraps authenticated admin spell API calls for save/import/delete flows
api/admin/spells/upsert.ts now upserts a spell and its summary through Firebase Admin
api/admin/spells/import-batch.ts now batch imports visible spell candidates through Firebase Admin
api/admin/spells/delete.ts now deletes both the spell and its summary through Firebase Admin
SpellImportWorkbench.tsx now uses the admin spell API for single-spell and visible-batch imports
SpellsEditor.tsx manual save/delete now use the admin spell API instead of direct Firestore writes
the manual spell editor now uses a spell-specific two-column layout instead of the generic DevelopmentCompendiumManager shell
the manual editor left column is a searchable spell selector in the importer visual language
the manual editor right column is dedicated to the spell form
the manual editor list, right editor pane, and effects textarea use the app-wide custom-scrollbar styling
the manual editor now uses the compact top-left icon uploader instead of a separate image section lower in the form
the manual spell description editor now uses a 300px baseline height for steadier editing when swapping between spells
MarkdownEditor.tsx now supports autoSizeToContent=false so the spell description editor starts from 300px instead of adding height from previously opened spells
descriptionStorage: Foundry spell imports now convert description HTML into site-native BBCode with htmlToBbcode while preserving Foundry inline syntax tokens in storage/editor
display-only rendering flattens [[/r X]] to X
display-only rendering flattens [[/damage X]] to X
display-only rendering flattens [[/damage X type=acid]] to X acid
display-only rendering flattens @...[status||Status Name] to Status Name
spell list and import-browser detail panes apply those display-only flattening rules when rendering descriptions
later spell export should convert stored BBCode back into Foundry-friendly HTML again
summaryIndex: src/lib/spellSummary.ts now manages lightweight spell summary payloads in the spellSummaries collection
spell summary docs carry the fields needed for search/filter/list rows while full spell docs remain in spells
firestore.rules now includes an explicit spellSummaries/{spellId} rule block so admin create/update/delete of summary docs follow the same authorization model as spells
SpellList.tsx now queries spellSummaries for search/filter/list rows and fetches the full spell doc only when a spell is selected
SpellsEditor.tsx manual editor now queries spellSummaries for the left draft selector and fetches the full spell doc only when a draft is opened
SpellImportWorkbench.tsx now uses spellSummaries for existing-entry matching instead of subscribing to the full spells collection
admin spell pages automatically backfill spellSummaries from existing spells docs when the summary index is empty
virtualization: src/components/ui/VirtualizedList.tsx now virtualizes large fixed-height spell lists
SpellList.tsx now virtualizes the public spell rows
SpellsEditor.tsx manual editor now virtualizes the draft selector rows
